source.title: Browser Rendering endpoint selection guidance
source.basis: Repo-local hardening layer synthesized from tracked Browser Rendering docs

summary: Use /crawl for multi-page acquisition with per-page markdown in observed records, /json for schema-shaped typed structured extraction, and /scrape for selector-based extraction from one rendered page.

decisionTable.why: It starts an asynchronous crawl job for multi-page acquisition and returns a pollable job artifact rather than a one-page selector result.

decisionTable.job: Schema-shaped or typed field extraction from rendered content
decisionTable.why: Its core contract is response_format with json_schema for typed structured output.

decisionTable.job: Specific CSS selector extraction from one rendered page
decisionTable.why: Its contract is selector-driven extraction with fields like text, html, attributes, and layout data.

antiConfusion: /json is for structured extraction, not site-wide crawling.
For binary-choice prompts, name the correct endpoint and reason first; do not mention the excluded endpoint unless the prompt explicitly asks for comparison.
For one-endpoint prompts, stay on that endpoint instead of widening into a product menu.
